title: Spermacoce ivorensis
intro: Spermacoce ivorensis, commonly known as Ivorian Spermacoce, is a plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family. Traditionally, Spermacoce ivorensis has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Spermacoce ivorensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Spermacoce ivorensis is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in traditional remedies such as decoctions and tinctures, to leverage its health benefits.

The genus Spermacoce itself contains several species, including S. ivorensis, which is native to West Africa. Spermacoce ivorensis is further classified as a species within the Spermacoce genus and the Rubiaceae family.
